title: Carpoceras hastulatum
intro: Carpoceras hastulatum is a type of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. Traditionally, Carpoceras hastulatum has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Carpoceras hastulatum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of extracts, tinctures, and ointments to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Carpoceras hastulatum is classified as a genus of brachiopod, which is a type of marine animal that belongs to the phylum Brachiopoda. The class of brachiopods to which Carpoceras hastulatum belongs is Strophomenata. This class is part of the larger group of marine animals known as articulate brachiopods. Within the phylum Brachiopoda, Carpoceras hastulatum would be categorized as a type of ancient brachiopod, specifically a strophomenate. The exact classification of Carpoceras hastulatum may vary depending on the source, but it is generally classified within the phylum Brachiopoda.
